These courses are provided across the Biochemistry and various other streams. At the UG level, the college offers three-year BSc courses to students. At the PG level, the college offers a two-year MSc in three specialisations. The college also offers Advanced Diploma in Bioinformatics (ADB) courses to students. faqs.answer: A:    Yes, BVP CET is required for B Sc. at Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Information Technology and Biotechnology. Rajiv Gandhi Institute of IT and Biotechnology (RGITBT) was established in 2003 in Pune, Maharashtra. It is a constituent unit of Bharati Vidyapeeth (Deemed to be University), Pune. The Institute offers UG, PG, and Diploma courses such as BSc, MSc, and Advanced Diploma in Bioinformatics (ADB). The campus comprises seven departments, including Biochemical Sciences, Microbial Biotechnology, Plant Biotechnology, Cell and Molecular Biology, Bioinformatics, Medical Biotechnology, and Library Sciences. As per my information Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Information Technology and Biotechnology does not offer MBA programme it primary offers -:M.Sc. (3 courses) B.Sc. (1 course) PG Diploma (1 course)The information can anyhow differ from what was available on the website, Thus it is advisable to visit the college website or contact the concerned department for the same.
faqs.question: Is BVP CET required for B Sc. at Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Information Technology and Biotechnology, Bharati Vidyapeeth University?

faqs.answer: A:    Candidates seeking admission to BSc at RGITBT Pune must appear for the BV-ABOP entrance test conudcted by Bharati Vidyapeeth. The last date to apply for the entrance test varies from year to year. In 2024, the exam was conducted in two phases. The last dates to apply for Phase 1 and 2 of the BV-ABOP were June 1,  2024, and June 13,  2024, respectively.

individual_reviews.course: M.Sc. in Bioinformatics - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely satisfied with the college experience and supportive faculty members.

content.Placements: The placement rate is 75 percent with top offers from companies like Infosys and Apelo Consultancy. Packages range from INR 3.5 LPA to 20 LPA with an average of INR 8 LPA. Around 70 percent of students secured internships with firms such as IBM, Wipro, and Biocon. Roles offered included Data Scientist, Junior Research Fellowship, and Data Analyst. The placement support is excellent.
content.Infrastructure: The department is equipped with Wi-Fi, smart classrooms, modern labs, and a well-stocked library. The hostels are clean with Wi-Fi, decent food in the mess and an on call doctor. Sports facilities include basketball, badminton, table tennis and a gym for student recreation.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. in Biotechnology - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: Great faculty, good lab structure, and a clean and beautiful campus.

content.Placements: Placements were not offered. Most students apply through teachers and mostly get opportunities at the Serum Institute. Teachers mostly communicate random opportunities via class groups on WhatsApp. Most students who got placed got 3 LPA. Most students pursue M.Sc. and M.Sc.-PhD. after the course.
content.Infrastructure: The hostel facility was good, and the mess had great facilities. Regular bus services are provided to commute between the hostel and college. The area is clean and well-maintained. Medical stores, stationary stores and easily accessible locations.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. in Biotechnology - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: I would rate the campus with above 3 stars out of 5 in all aspects because it deserves it.

content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is good if you talk about college building, design, and internal environment but there your experience may be low in terms of college labs and classrooms. Quality of facilities in the hostel is not much more good but if talk about restrictions, security and all then it may win your heart. Mess food is good enough.

placement_data.title: Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Information Technology and Biotechnology Placement Overview 2023
paragraphs: As per the NIRF report 2024 released by Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Information Technology and Biotechnology, the median package offered during RGITBT PG 2-year placements 2023 stood at INR 3.34 LPA . Further, a total of 73 students were placed and 1 student opted for higher studies out of total 94 students during PG 2-year placements 2023. Earlier, according to the NIRF report 2022, a total of 43 out of 100 MSc students were placed during Rajiv Gandhi Pune placement 2021 . The median package offered to MSc students was INR 2.23 LPA during RGITBT placement 2021 .

faqs.question: What are the career prospects after completing B.Sc at RGITBT Pune?
faqs.answer: RGITBT Pune offers a BSc in Biotechnology. On completion of their BSc degree in Biotechnology, graduates can take up higher education with courses like MSc , MS, MBA, etc. For someone who wants to get employed right after their graduation, some career prospects have been listed below: Scientist/Researcher Biotechnologist Teacher/Educator Employment in a Medical colleges, Hospitals, Research Institutes, Healthcare Organisations, etc. Visit BSc (Biotechnology) career scope for more details.

fees_and_eligibility.overview: C. U. Shah University provides Full Time programs at the UG and PG levels. These courses are available in multiple streams like Engineering, Medicine & Health Sciences and Business & Management Studies, and have a fee structure of around INR 78,000 to 4,84,000. UG programs include B.E. / B.Tech, B.Sc. and BCA. Further, the PG programs include M.E./M.Tech, MBA/PGDM and M.Sc..

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering - Batch of 2016
reviews_list.review_title: There's a lack of practical education so that students are facing difficulties to get placed.

review_content.Placements: Only one company, i.e., TCS visited the campus for placement drives conducted to offer placement opportunities for final year students. Only 2-3 students were placed throughout the entire placement process. The salary offered to students is ranged between Rs.10,000-15,000 per month. The rest of the students have to find a job by themselves.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of this institute is good. It has a library. The entire campus is Wi-Fi enabled. There is a huge sports ground behind the college. Canteen facilities are not good. There are only a few food choices offered here, and even the snack options are limited. Cleanliness is not maintained on the campus. Classrooms are quite good. Labs are not much used by our faculty; they are just for namesake. Moreover, students are not aware of the concepts because faculty members are not trying to teach practical applications of various concepts.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Computer Engineering - Batch of 2015
reviews_list.review_title: Disappointed, concerning placements in 2015.

review_content.Placements: During the final year of my graduation, IT companies like TCS and Zero Choas visited the college to conduct placements for the students of computer science and IT branch. For the students studying mechanical and electrical branches, many companies visited the college during our placement season. TCS hardly selected 6 to 7 students out of 110 to 120 students. TCS generally offers a package of 3.5 LPA. Students were offered assistant software engineer role. Internships were not provided by the college. Placement cell was available, but it was not much useful.

overview_faqs.question: Is Computer Engineering a good choice for career?
overview_faqs.answer: Yes, CSE (full form Computer Science and Engineering) is a good career choice if you are aspiring for a good salary package as a fresher. A Computer Engineering graduate's average salary in India is approx. INR 6 lakh and higher. Also, owing to the exponential rise in AI & ML concept), CSE course gradutes have got a competitive job market and abundant opportunities for career growth.

review_summary.college_name: C. U. Shah University Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: Master of Social Work (MSW) - Batch of 2019
individual_reviews.title: It is the best college in Gujarat.

content.Placements: Hindustan Unilever, Adani, Reliance, Aditya Birla, Saint Gobain, and many other companies visit our campus for recruitment. Recruitment depends on the policies of companies, but mostly there is an aptitude test to reach the interview. The placement percentage is 70%–75%.
content.Infrastructure: The hostel facility at this college is good. Every room has three numbers and has three beds for each member. There is a common bathroom for the students. The meal quality is not up to the mark. An improvement is necessary in the quality of meals. The menu is good. Wi-Fi is also provided in the hostel.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Engineering - Batch of 2017
individual_reviews.title: It is a good college, but they need to improve according to real industry practice.

content.Placements: Approximately 20% of the students are placed. TCS is the top recruiting company of our college which offered a salary package of 2LPA. In the computer engineering branch, 80% of the students are doing an internship programme.
content.Infrastructure: Facilities on campus are good, and Wi-Fi and hostel facilities are available here. The location is good, which is nearby the high way. Hostel facilities are average as compared to landmarks.
content.Faculty: Professors in our college have good knowledge, and labs practical are conducted here. All the professors are well-skilled, and they are teaching according to all course in industrial knowledge. All courses in need to improve according to the latest industrial practice and theory because syllabus knowledge is not enough for the real world.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering - Batch of 2016
individual_reviews.title: There's a lack of practical education so that students are facing difficulties to get placed.

content.Placements: Only one company, i.e., TCS visited the campus for placement drives conducted to offer placement opportunities for final year students. Only 2-3 students were placed throughout the entire placement process. The salary offered to students is ranged between Rs.10,000-15,000 per month. The rest of the students have to find a job by themselves.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of this institute is good. It has a library. The entire campus is Wi-Fi enabled. There is a huge sports ground behind the college. Canteen facilities are not good. There are only a few food choices offered here, and even the snack options are limited. Cleanliness is not maintained on the campus. Classrooms are quite good. Labs are not much used by our faculty; they are just for namesake. Moreover, students are not aware of the concepts because faculty members are not trying to teach practical applications of various concepts.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Engineering - Batch of 2015
individual_reviews.title: Disappointed, concerning placements in 2015.

content.Placements: During the final year of my graduation, IT companies like TCS and Zero Choas visited the college to conduct placements for the students of computer science and IT branch. For the students studying mechanical and electrical branches, many companies visited the college during our placement season. TCS hardly selected 6 to 7 students out of 110 to 120 students. TCS generally offers a package of 3.5 LPA. Students were offered assistant software engineer role. Internships were not provided by the college. Placement cell was available, but it was not much useful.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Electronics and Communication Engineering - Batch of 2013
individual_reviews.title: I am dissatisfied with the college because of placements.

content.Placements: Placements were not good for the students of our batch and our senior batch. Rarely 2 to 3 companies visited our college for placements. Only 2% to 3% of the students got placed. The average salary offered was Rs. 25,000 per month. TCS visited the campus for placements, and students were recruited for technical support engineer role. The college doesn't provide any internship opportunities. A placement cell was also available in our college, but it was not useful.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college was excellent. They provided Wi-Fi facilities for limited periods. Labs were well-maintained with every instrument. The library was full of books but not useful for students because they only issue 2 books at a time. Sports facilities were not good. There was a certain space for sports and outdoor activities. They also provide computer systems in the lab. Classrooms were very clean with big blackboard, projector and benches. The canteen was not that good. Students can only have wafers, biscuits and tea. There is no hostel on the campus. I did not face any issues related to infrastructure during my tenure.

highlights.summary:
NIRF 2025 Rankings are out. As per the latest NIRF 2025 rankings, Holy Cross College ranks #41 under the category 'College'.

The TANCET 2026 application form is live until Apr 10, 2026, for Holy Cross College admission to the MBA course. Also, the TANCET exam 2026 is scheduled for May 9, 2026.

placements.overview:
Holy Cross College is a private institute, established in 1923 in Tiruchirappalli, Tamil Nadu. Holy Cross College is approved by AICTE and offers a total of 55 courses at UG, PG and Certificate levels in 10 specialisations such as Bioinformatics, Biotechnology, Botany, Chemistry and Computer Science. Furthermore, it offer courses such as MBA/PGDM, B.Sc., M.Sc., MCA and BCA in Full Time mode. Holy Cross College has an annual seat intake of 270 for all the programmes. It offers courses, in streams such as Medicine & Health Sciences, Business & Management Studies, IT & Software, Accounting & Commerce, Mass Communication & Media and Humanities & Social Sciences. As per the recent Holy Cross College placement records, the average median salary at the Institute is around INR 2,38,732. The institute has a safe and well-equipped campus with all the advanced facilities.

Q. Which courses does Holy Cross College offer?

reviews_list.batch_info: M.Sc. in Botany with Specialisation in Plant Biotechnology - Batch of 2022
reviews_list.review_title: Overall, best college for M.Sc. in botany course.

review_content.Placements: Approximately 90% of the students participated in our study. Top recruitment companies that visited our college for recruitment were Patanjali, Serum India Ltd, Bharat Biotech, etc. The highest salary was 12 LPA, and the lowest salary was 2 LPA. College offered an internship based on your CGPA. Overall, it was good. Top jobs were ecologist, horticulturist, and so on.
review_content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi was excellent with fast speed. Labs were good with a wide range of equipment, and instructors were also good. Classrooms were large and plentiful. There was a proper library. Food was good, and we had a separate medical center with visiting doctors from Apollo. We had sports including badminton, squash, table tennis, soccer, swimming pool, and many more. Our college had a positive campus life and culture for overall student achievements.

reviews_list.batch_info: Masters of Social Work - Batch of 2020
reviews_list.review_title: Overall, it is the best college for Master's of Social Work course.

review_content.Placements: About 87% of students get placed. The maximum package offered is 25 LPA, and the average package is 12 LPA. It has a tie-up with more than 100 companies. It invites more than 60 companies every year. All students, according to their skills and abilities, get opportunities for internships. The top recruiting companies for our course are HelpAge India, ITC, CVS Health, etc. High-level roles like social worker, case manager, etc., are offered here.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college amenities include accommodation for students. Classrooms are cozy and entirely climate-controlled. Classrooms feature a charging station for laptops to facilitate hands-on learning experiences. Wi-Fi access is provided across the campus. Amenities at the hostel are fantastic. Hostels atmosphere is supportive of education, and there is no aggression.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Sc. in Biotechnology - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: If my college and my course are very satisfied.

review_content.Placements: 90% of students got placed from my course. The highest package is Rs. 30,000 per month, the lowest is Rs. 16,000 per month, and the average is Rs. 20,000- Rs. 25,000 per month. 90% of students got placed in the internships from my course. HR, head of the department, and assistant manager these roles can be offered in my course.
review_content.Infrastructure: In my college and my course there are various types of staff who can be associated. The Wi-Fi and the lab facilities are good and the classrooms and library infrastructure are very good. The food quality is good and neat. Medical facilities are available 24/7. The sports and games facilities are good.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.A. in English Literature (Communicative English) - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: It has good placements and good work.

review_content.Placements: 80% of students get placed in my course. There are two packages, government-applied and self-finance. Government applications include scholarships for minorities, etc. Many popular companies recruit students from our college like Omega, V-Dart, etc. 70% of students got internships from companies. Many scholars are offering placements as professors in college itself.
review_content.Infrastructure: There is a language lab for enhancing our English language speaking. There is a library with a Wi-Fi connection and a canteen for students and faculty members. It has clean restrooms. Rapha clinic is on the campus for medical emergencies which is very useful for hostel students as well as day scholars. It has 17 clubs and also sports like football, volleyball, basketball, shot put, javelin throw, athletes, etc. We get healthy food in the hostel mess and it also provides a noon-meal scheme for Day scholars.

reviews_list.batch_info: Masters of Social Work - Batch of 2020
reviews_list.review_title: Overall the best college for master's of social work.

review_content.Placements: In our study, almost 76% of pupils made progress. The average salary package offered is 16 LPA. The highest salary package offered is 37 LPA. The lowest salary package offered is 7 LPA. The Red Cross, Oxfam, Unicef, and others are top recruiters. Program manager and social worker are among the top positions available. Internships are obtained by about 60% of students.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure at our college is outstanding. Our college gives us everything we need. Classrooms have projectors. The library is amazing. Water and electricity are available. The food served in the restaurant is good and clean. They offer fitness and sports services. Bathrooms are fine. The living room is nice.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Sc. in Mathematics - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: Path towards the successful life.

review_content.Placements: There are more companies like Omega, TCS, etc., are visit for campus drives. The average salary package offered ranges from 3 LPA - 6 LPA. Students who get above 75% can easily get placements in companies. College fee is affordable for all types of students.
review_content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure of the college and hostel is quite good. Library contains 130,843 books and 148 journals. They give importance to sports activities also. There are up to 5-8 students per room in the hostel. They provide healthy food and follow their routine menu.

answer.text: Different reasons for flunking nursing classes are so numerous, yet unique to each and every person. Some of main causes that normally lead to one experiencing academic difficulties in nursing classes are:Difficulty understanding concepts: nursing programs are demanding, and it is genuinely tough to grasp very complicated medical concepts, procedures, and pharmacology.Clinical performance problems: These can be concerns for students who are experiencing complications with clinical skills or interpersonal communications.Poor study habits: One of most important study skills in any nursing program. A student who does not have proper study habits, time management, or organizational skills might not manage to keep pace according to coursework.Overwhelmed by workload: workload, in particular, can be intense in nursing programs, leaving students overwhelmed as to what to learn and acquire, complete as assignments, and spend in clinical hours.

lists: Difficulty understanding concepts: nursing programs are demanding, and it is genuinely tough to grasp very complicated medical concepts, procedures, and pharmacology.
Clinical performance problems: These can be concerns for students who are experiencing complications with clinical skills or interpersonal communications.

Poor study habits: One of most important study skills in any nursing program. A student who does not have proper study habits, time management, or organizational skills might not manage to keep pace according to coursework.

review_summary.college_name: Siddhakala Ayurved Mahavidyalaya Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: BAMS - Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Really good infrastructure.

content.Placements: Students from our college get hired by different hospitals. Nearly all college students are hardworking and talented, so they secure jobs. One hundred percent of all students get placed in various hospitals after the course, with good packages. All students must complete a compulsory internship at different hospitals. The lowest package is around 30,000 per month, and the highest is about 80,000 per month.
content.Infrastructure: The college infrastructure is one of the best, with excellent lecture halls equipped with digital equipment. The library has Wi-Fi, and there is also a seminar hall. The hostel is average, not luxurious, and the hostel food is not very good. For sports, there is a playground and a tennis court.
